prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that explains adaptive quadtree refinement. Start with one square region, split it into four cells, zoom the camera into the active cell, refine that cell again, and finish by zooming back out with a caption that explains why computation is spent only where detail is needed.
completion:
from manim import *

class QuadtreeCameraRefinementStory(MovingCameraScene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Adaptive Quadtree Refinement", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        region = Square(side_length=4.0, color=BLUE_D).shift(LEFT * 0.8 + DOWN * 0.2)
        vertical = Line(region.get_top(), region.get_bottom(), color=BLUE_B)
        horizontal = Line(region.get_left(), region.get_right(), color=BLUE_B)
        first_split = VGroup(vertical, horizontal)

        cell_centers = [
            region.get_center() + LEFT + UP,
            region.get_center() + RIGHT + UP,
            region.get_center() + LEFT + DOWN,
            region.get_center() + RIGHT + DOWN,
        ]
        labels = VGroup(*[Text(str(i + 1), font_size=24, color=GRAY_A).move_to(point) for i, point in enumerate(cell_centers)])
        active = Square(side_length=2.0, color=YELLOW_D).move_to(cell_centers[1])
        active_label = Text("active error pocket", font_size=24, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(active, RIGHT, buff=0.35)

        fine_vertical = Line(active.get_top(), active.get_bottom(), color=GREEN_D)
        fine_horizontal = Line(active.get_left(), active.get_right(), color=GREEN_D)
        fine_split = VGroup(fine_vertical, fine_horizontal)
        fine_cells = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=1.0, color=GREEN_D).move_to(active.get_center() + dx * RIGHT + dy * UP)
            for dx in (-0.5, 0.5)
            for dy in (-0.5, 0.5)
        ])
        budget_note = Text("More cells only where the signal changes fast", font_size=26, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        budget_panel = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(budget_note, fill_opacity=0.85, buff=0.16), budget_note)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(region), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(first_split), FadeIn(labels), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(Create(active), FadeIn(active_label), run_time=2)
        self.camera.frame.save_state()
        self.play(self.camera.frame.animate.set(width=4.0).move_to(active), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(fine_split), FadeIn(fine_cells), run_time=3)
        self.play(Indicate(fine_cells, color=GREEN_A), run_time=2)
        self.play(Restore(self.camera.frame), FadeIn(budget_panel), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create an 18-second Manim scene that explains one Kalman filter correction. Show a prediction point, a noisy measurement, a residual line between them, a confidence gate, and a zoom window around the residual so the final corrected estimate is readable.
completion:
from manim import *

class KalmanZoomResidualGate(ZoomedScene):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(
            zoom_factor=0.45,
            zoomed_display_height=2.3,
            zoomed_display_width=3.8,
            image_frame_stroke_width=6,
            zoomed_camera_config={"default_frame_stroke_width": 3},
            **kwargs,
        )

    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Kalman Correction: Residual Gate", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-4, 4, 1], y_range=[-2.5, 2.5, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.22})
        prediction = Dot(LEFT * 1.7 + DOWN * 0.2, color=BLUE_D, radius=0.08)
        measurement = Dot(RIGHT * 1.4 + UP * 0.7, color=YELLOW_D, radius=0.08)
        gate = Circle(radius=1.05, color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=3).move_to(measurement)
        alpha = ValueTracker(0.0)

        estimate = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(
            interpolate(prediction.get_center(), measurement.get_center(), 0.62 * alpha.get_value()),
            color=GREEN_D,
            radius=0.09,
        ))
        residual = always_redraw(lambda: Line(prediction.get_center(), measurement.get_center(), color=RED_D))
        correction = always_redraw(lambda: Arrow(prediction.get_center(), estimate.get_center(), buff=0.05, color=GREEN_D))
        labels = VGroup(
            Text("prediction", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(prediction, DOWN),
            Text("measurement", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(measurement, UP),
            Text("corrected estimate", font_size=22, color=GREEN_D).to_edge(DOWN),
        )

        frame = self.zoomed_camera.frame
        frame.move_to((prediction.get_center() + measurement.get_center()) / 2)
        frame.set_color(PURPLE)
        self.zoomed_display.shift(DOWN * 1.9)
        self.zoomed_display.display_frame.set_color(PURPLE)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(prediction), FadeIn(measurement), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(gate), Create(residual), FadeIn(labels[:2]), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(frame), run_time=2)
        self.activate_zooming()
        self.play(self.get_zoomed_display_pop_out_animation(), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(alpha.animate.set_value(1.0), Create(correction), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(labels[2]), Indicate(estimate, color=GREEN_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1.5)

prompt: Create an 18-second Manim scene that explains how Riemann-sum error shrinks. Plot a smooth curve on axes, show a coarse set of rectangles, transform them into a finer set, add a brace marking one rectangle width, and display a numeric error readout that decreases.
completion:
from manim import *

class RiemannErrorShrinkStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Riemann error shrinks with narrower rectangles", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 4, 1],
            y_range=[0, 5, 1],
            x_length=7.0,
            y_length=4.2,
            tips=False,
            axis_config={"include_numbers": True},
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.25)

        def curve_fn(x):
            return 0.28 * (x - 1.0) ** 3 + 2.2

        curve = axes.plot(curve_fn, x_range=[0.25, 3.75], color=BLUE_D, stroke_width=5)
        coarse = axes.get_riemann_rectangles(curve, x_range=[0.4, 3.6], dx=0.8, color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.45)
        fine = axes.get_riemann_rectangles(curve, x_range=[0.4, 3.6], dx=0.32, color=GREEN_D, fill_opacity=0.42)
        width_brace = Brace(coarse[1], DOWN, color=YELLOW_D)
        width_label = Text("wide dx", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(width_brace, DOWN, buff=0.12)
        error_value = DecimalNumber(0.48, num_decimal_places=2, font_size=30, color=YELLOW_D)
        error_label = Text("area error", font_size=24, color=WHITE).next_to(error_value, LEFT, buff=0.15)
        error_panel = VGroup(error_label, error_value).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.15).to_corner(UR).shift(DOWN * 0.55)
        caption = Text("Finer partitions track the curve more closely.", font_size=24, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_panel = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.86, buff=0.16), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(axes), Create(curve), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(coarse), GrowFromCenter(width_brace), FadeIn(width_label), FadeIn(error_panel), run_time=3)
        self.play(Transform(coarse, fine), FadeOut(width_brace), FadeOut(width_label), error_value.animate.set_value(0.14).set_color(GREEN_D), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_panel), Indicate(coarse, color=GREEN_A), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 16-second Manim scene that visualizes a directional antenna pattern on a polar plane. Show the polar grid, animate the main lobe sweeping slightly, add side-lobe markers, and finish with a label explaining that gain is directional rather than uniform.
completion:
from manim import *

class PolarAntennaLobeScan(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Directional Antenna Gain", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = PolarPlane(radius_max=3.0, size=5.8, background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.28}).add_coordinates()
        plane.shift(DOWN * 0.2)
        phase = ValueTracker(-0.35)
        pattern = always_redraw(lambda: plane.plot_polar_graph(
            lambda theta: 1.2 + 1.1 * max(0, np.cos(theta - phase.get_value())) ** 4 + 0.25 * np.cos(4 * theta) ** 2,
            theta_range=[0, TAU],
            color=YELLOW_D,
            stroke_width=5,
        ))
        main_arrow = always_redraw(lambda: Arrow(
            plane.polar_to_point(0.0, 0.0),
            plane.polar_to_point(phase.get_value(), 2.75),
            buff=0,
            color=GREEN_D,
        ))
        side_notes = VGroup(
            Dot(plane.polar_to_point(1.25, 1.45), color=BLUE_D),
            Dot(plane.polar_to_point(-1.25, 1.45), color=BLUE_D),
            Text("side lobes", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).next_to(plane, RIGHT, buff=0.35),
        )
        note = Text("Gain changes with direction", font_size=26, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        note_panel = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(note, fill_opacity=0.85, buff=0.16), note)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(pattern), GrowArrow(main_arrow), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(side_notes), run_time=2)
        self.play(phase.animate.set_value(0.45), run_time=4, rate_func=there_and_back)
        self.play(FadeIn(note_panel), Indicate(pattern, color=YELLOW_A), run_time=2)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 20-second Manim scene that explains a Jacobian as a local linear map. Show a point on a plane, a small input arrow, transform the formula with matching LaTeX, display a 2x2 matrix, and then show the output arrow produced by that local linearization.
completion:
from manim import *

class TransformMatchingJacobianStory(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Jacobian = local linear map", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        plane = NumberPlane(x_range=[-3, 3, 1], y_range=[-2, 2, 1], background_line_style={"stroke_opacity": 0.24}).shift(LEFT * 1.7)
        base = Dot(plane.c2p(0.8, 0.5), color=YELLOW_D)
        input_vec = Arrow(base.get_center(), base.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.9 + UP * 0.35, buff=0, color=BLUE_D)
        output_vec = Arrow(base.get_center(), base.get_center() + RIGHT * 0.55 + UP * 0.95, buff=0, color=GREEN_D)
        input_label = MathTex(r"d\vec{x}", color=BLUE_D).next_to(input_vec, DOWN, buff=0.12)
        output_label = MathTex(r"J d\vec{x}", color=GREEN_D).next_to(output_vec, RIGHT, buff=0.12)

        formula_a = MathTex(r"f(\vec{x}+d\vec{x}) \approx f(\vec{x}) + J d\vec{x}", font_size=34).to_edge(DOWN)
        formula_b = MathTex(r"d\vec{y} = J d\vec{x}", font_size=38).to_edge(DOWN)
        matrix = Matrix([["1.2", "-0.4"], ["0.5", "0.9"]]).scale(0.72).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 0.35)
        matrix_title = Text("local matrix", font_size=24, color=WHITE).next_to(matrix, UP, buff=0.2)
        box = SurroundingRectangle(matrix, color=GREEN_D, buff=0.18)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(plane), FadeIn(base), run_time=3)
        self.play(GrowArrow(input_vec), FadeIn(input_label), FadeIn(formula_a), run_time=3)
        self.play(TransformMatchingTex(formula_a, formula_b), FadeIn(matrix), FadeIn(matrix_title), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(box), TransformFromCopy(input_vec, output_vec), FadeIn(output_label), run_time=4)
        self.play(Indicate(matrix, color=GREEN_A), Indicate(output_vec, color=GREEN_A), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 22-second Manim scene that traces one attention step from code to matrix output. Show a short Python-like pseudocode block, a 3x3 score matrix, highlight one query row, copy that row into a weighted value vector, and end with a compact caption that connects the code line to the matrix operation.
completion:
from manim import *

class CodeMatrixAttentionTrace(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Attention: one query row", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        snippet = Code(
            code_string="scores = Q @ K.T\nweights = softmax(scores)\nout = weights @ V",
            language="Python",
            formatter_style="monokai",
            add_line_numbers=False,
            background="window",
        ).scale(0.62).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.55).shift(UP * 0.2)
        scores = Matrix([["0.1", "1.4", "0.3"], ["0.7", "0.2", "1.1"], ["1.2", "0.4", "0.5"]]).scale(0.62).shift(RIGHT * 1.1 + UP * 0.35)
        values = Matrix([["v_1"], ["v_2"], ["v_3"]]).scale(0.62).next_to(scores, RIGHT, buff=0.9)
        row_box = SurroundingRectangle(scores.get_rows()[1], color=YELLOW_D, buff=0.08)
        value_box = SurroundingRectangle(values, color=GREEN_D, buff=0.1)
        row_label = Text("query 2 weights", font_size=22, color=YELLOW_D).next_to(row_box, DOWN, buff=0.18)
        out_token = VGroup(
            RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.08, width=1.3, height=0.7, color=GREEN_D, fill_color=GREEN_E, fill_opacity=0.25),
            MathTex(r"out_2", color=GREEN_D),
        ).arrange().next_to(values, DOWN, buff=0.55)
        caption = Text("The highlighted code line becomes a weighted sum over value vectors.", font_size=22, color=WHITE).to_edge(DOWN)
        caption_panel = VGroup(BackgroundRectangle(caption, fill_opacity=0.86, buff=0.16), caption)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(snippet), run_time=3)
        self.play(FadeIn(scores), FadeIn(values), run_time=3)
        self.play(Create(row_box), FadeIn(row_label), run_time=2.5)
        self.play(Create(value_box), TransformFromCopy(scores.get_rows()[1], out_token), run_time=4)
        self.play(FadeIn(caption_panel), Indicate(out_token, color=GREEN_A), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)
